calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Baulsier, via ZOOM, for Jennifer Mac Kenzie, via ZOOM, Executor; Gene Benitez, via ZOOM, for Edwin Dyer, Objector.

Mr. Benitez confirms that there is a disagreement regarding the amount owed to the trust which he reports to be $6,158.55. Ms. Baulsier offers oral advocacy regarding her position and acknowledges to the court of her oversights handling this matter and points to her attempts to correct them.

The Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Andam. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Baulsier appeared by telephone for and with Jennifer Mac Kenzie by zoom.

The Court noted that this matter was on calendar in November of 2020, that a directive was issued to Ms. Baulsier and Ms. Mac Kenzie as to the corpus of the estate that were distributed without a Court order and that arrangements were to be made to restore the assets. Ms. Baulsier stated that they assembled what refunds were made by the Beneficiaries, that she cannot type due to an injury and she has no secretary and that it will take eight weeks for her to be in a condition to do anything. Ms. Mac Kenzie stated that she opened an estate account at Bank of the West with $92,474.00. The Court noted that it was contemplated that the corpus of the estate was going to be $625,000.00. Ms. Baulsier stated that nothing else can be assembled at this time, that she believes there has been an error in the calculation of the Trustee, but that issue has not been reviewed and requested a continuance until April. The Court stated that April would be a long continuance, that it has no confidence in the parties and that perhaps Ms. Baulsier needs to proceed under an ADA accommodation. The Petition to Close Estate, etc. is continued to March 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that it is reluctantly continuing the matter and that is wants to hear what progress has been made.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-17-2020 Continued To Feb-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Baulsier appeared by zoom for and with Jennifer Mac Kenzie by zoom.

The Court stated that it reviewed the submission of the earlier spousal property petition, that the just of that petition was a request to the Court to transfer the property to the trust, and that the Court said no, and the Parties were told to follow the proper steps. The Court also stated that nothing was put into Alicia or Keith's estate when it should have been, and that except for the $12,000.00 that was held in the Attorney client trust account which should not have happened, the estate was de void of any assets because everything was given to Edgar as the Trustee. In addition, the Court spoke of the Executor having signed the document titled "Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative" in which she acknowledges her responsibility to not co-mingle estate assets with anyone else's assets, or make any distributions without a Court order, raised the issue of Counsel having brought cases in this County in the past and knowing not to take any fees from an estate without a Court order, and questions how this happened and how this can be fixed. Ms. Baulsier stated that she believes in good faith that her Client was acting under her Independent Administration of Estate Act authority and didn't believe that the funds were co-mingled in escrow, and asked the Court to allow it. Ms. Baulsier also stated that they can transfer funds to an estate account and that $3,000.00 is being held by the Trustee and are available. The Court noted that Keith's will said that estate distribution was to be consistent with Article 7 section 3. Ms. Bauliser stated that the surviving Spouse was deceased before the Wife's estate was administered and that under the code it would be distributed to his estate which was the trust. The Court stated that this is incorrect. The Court orders the following: Ms. Bauliser is ordered to return to the Executor all amounts that were paid to her. The Executor shall deposit the proceeds into an appropriate account and refrain from making any distributions. Counsel and the Executor are to recover all of the improper distributions that were made from Alicia's estate. The Executor and Counsel must amend the petition to include a recording of all transactions/an accounting. Ms. Baulsier and Ms. Mac Kenzie are directed to make best efforts to recover assets. Any amounts that are not restored will be considered as losses to the estate in figuring out statutory fees. The Petition to Close Estate, etc. is continued to February 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Baulsier and Ms. Mac Kenzie are ordered to be present.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-01-2019 Continued To Jul-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Baulsier for Petitioner.
Magda Mackenzie and Daniel Mackenzie also present.

The Court stated that it is not at all clear that this is a proper procedure or that the Petitioner is in a position to do so without appointment as the personal representative, and that there is no pending administration. Ms. Baulsier stated that the Spouse is deceased, that there is a relative that could be a personal representative and that when looking at code section 13500 her research indicates that the property would pass without administration. The Court informed Ms. Baulsier that this petition is deeply flawed in many ways and ultimately can not proceed, and that if she is going to pursue this, she will have to do it in a different way. The Petition for Spousal Property is denied without prejudice to proceeding in a proper fashion. The Court informed Counsel that if any further proceedings related to the estate of Alicia Hopkinson are to be filed, she is to use the same case number.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
